zh.Wortley, South Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭越可爾沃特利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas Wortley I於大約1378年出生在英國越可爾沃特利時，他的父親John WORTLEY21歲，母親Elizabeth de la Hay-1歲。他於1398年與Elizabeth Waterton在英國英格蘭越可爾沃特利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1448年1月22日之前在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.Wortley, South Yorkshire, England: 英国英格兰越可爾沃特利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicholas Wortley I于大约1378年出生在英国越可爾沃特利时，他的父亲John WORTLEY21岁，母亲Elizabeth de la Hay-1岁。他于1398年与Elizabeth Waterton在英国英格兰越可爾沃特利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1448年1月22日之前在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Wortley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Wortley in Tankersley (Yorkshire), or Lower, Upper, or New Wortley in Armley (Yorkshire), or (least likely) Wortley in Wotton under Edge (Gloucestershire). The place in Tankersley derives from Old English wyrt ‘plant, vegetable’ + lēah ‘wood, woodland clearing’. The place in Armley derives from an Old English personal name Wyrca or Weorca + Old English lēah. The Gloucestershire placename derives from Old English wyrt + lēah.

variants.name: Worley
meanings: habitational name with different origins. In the West Midlands this surname is most probably derived from Great or Little Wyrley in Norton Canes (Staffordshire). The placenames derive from Old English wīr ‘bog myrtle’ + lēah ‘wood, woodland clearing’. In the East Midlands, the name is possibly from Worlick in Ramsey (Huntingdonshire), the etymology of the which is uncertain. In northern England, possibly from Whirley in Over Alderley (Cheshire), which has the same etymology as the Staffordshire names. There may also be a further unlocated source of this habitational name in southern England.
possibly an irregular variant of   Wortley   or   Warley  .

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas Wortley I was born about 1378, in Wortley, Yorkshire, England, his father, John WORTLEY, was 21 and his mother, Elizabeth de la Hay, was -1. He married Elizabeth Waterton in 1398, in Wortley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. He died before 22 January 1448, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas Wortley I est né environ 1378, à Wortley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John WORTLEY, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de la Hay, avait -1 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Waterton en 1398, à Wortley, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé avant 22 janvier 1448, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicholas Wortley I um 1378 in Wortley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John WORTLEY 21 und seine Mutter Elizabeth de la Hay -1. Er hat 1398 in Wortley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth Waterton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb vor 22. Januar 1448 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Wortley I è nato il circa 1378 nel Wortley, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, John WORTLEY, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth de la Hay, aveva -1 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Waterton nel 1398, nel Wortley, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. Morì prima del 22 gennaio 1448, nel sua cittadina.

ja.Wortley, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアウオルトリー
ja.Wortley, South Yorkshire, England: イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウオルトリー
ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas Wortley Iが約1378年にイギリスヨークシュアイアウオルトリーで生まれた時，父John WORTLEYは21歳，母Elizabeth de la Hayは-1歳であった。1398年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウオルトリーでElizabeth Watertonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1448年1月22日以前に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas Wortley I(이)가 대략 1378년 영국 요르크실르 워틀리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John WORTLEY(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth de la Hay(은)는 -1세였다. 그는 1398년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 워틀리에서 Elizabeth Waterton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1448년 1월 22일 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Wortley I nasceu aproximadamente 1378, em Wortley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John WORTLEY, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de la Hay, tinha -1 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Waterton em 1398, em Wortley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 22 de janeiro de 1448, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas Wortley I родился около 1378 г. в местности вортли, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John WORTLEY было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de la Hay, -1. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Waterton в 1398 г. в местности вортли, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер перед 22 январь 1448 г. в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicholas Wortley I nació aproximadamente 1378, en Wortley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John WORTLEY, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Elizabeth de la Hay, tenía -1 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Waterton en 1398, en Wortley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió antes de 22 de enero de 1448, en su pueblo.
